origin.capability_match: Cancel malicious governance proposals during the timelock period

attack_surface.data_description: Maliciously crafted cancellation signal from compromised/colluding Guardians, or strategic failure to cancel a known malicious proposal.

security_gap.gap_description: The Guardian role, if a quorum is compromised or colluding, can abuse its cancellation power to subvert governance.

actor_risk.description: A compromised or colluding quorum of Guardians (5-of-9 multisig) abuses their intended privilege, effectively elevating their control over the governance outcome.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromise or collusion of a Guardian quorum directly subverts the intended governance safeguard and represents a significant centralization of power.

assumptions: A quorum (5 out of 9) of Guardian private keys can be simultaneously compromised, or their holders can collude maliciously.
There are insufficient rapid detection mechanisms or alternative (emergency) governance pathways to counteract malicious actions by a compromised Guardian quorum.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure robust operational security for Guardian keys, select diverse and reputable Guardian members, establish clear public criteria for cancellations, and consider community monitoring or alert systems for Guardian actions.

description: A compromised or colluding quorum of Guardians abuses their privileged role by cancelling legitimate governance proposals or by deliberately failing to cancel known malicious ones. This effectively elevates their power to manipulate governance outcomes, bypassing community consensus.

trace_source.matched_capability: Cancel malicious governance proposals during the timelock period
